profile.name: Shafayat Alam
profile.title: Mechanical Engineer · Roboticist
profile.bio: I'm a Mechanical Engineering student at Stony Brook University with experience across underwater robotics, aerospace systems, and computational fluid dynamics. My work spans the full stack — from soft actuator design and CFD investigations to launch vehicle subsystems and thermal control — always grounded in rigorous testing and analysis.
profile.education: B.E. Mechanical Engineering · Stony Brook University · 2026

entries.title: UNDERWATER SOFT ROBOTIC SYSTEM
what: Full-stack architecture of an underwater robotic system
Soft actuator design, chassis development, and sensor/electronics integration
how: Soft actuator design and dynamic characterization
ROS2-based control software and sensor integration
Systematic testing protocols across operating and transitioning media
results: Novel characterization of actuator dynamics across fluid media
Ongoing: full system integration from mechanical to software stack

entries.title: COMPETITIVE LAUNCH VEHICLE
what: Numerical characterization of launch vehicle flight envelope
Mechanical design, fabrication, and integration of competitive subsystems
how: Aerodynamic simulation and flight envelope analysis
Subsystem mechanical design and fabrication
Integration testing and ground verification
results: Aerodynamic behavior characterized across full flight envelope
Competitive subsystems fabricated, integrated, and verified

entries.title: COMPRESSIBLE CFD: TRAPEZOIDAL FIN AERODYNAMICS
what: Numerical investigation of trapezoidal fin aerodynamics in compressible flow
Meshing support for Triply Periodic Minimal Surface heat exchangers
how: Compressible RANS with Spalart-Allmaras turbulence closure
OpenFOAM on HPC cluster with parallel computing
Systematic geometric parameter sweep
results: 30.7% drag reduction identified at a 46% lift-to-drag ratio penalty
Quantified aerodynamic trade-space for fin geometry optimization
Advanced TPMS heat exchanger meshing methodology

entries.title: AUTONOMOUS AEROBOT THERMAL CONTROL SYSTEM
what: Thermal analysis for an autonomous planetary aerobot
Passive thermal control system design following the NASA mission lifecycle
how: Churchill-Bernstein convection models and heat flow mapping
Variable geometry radiator with low emissivity coating (ε = 0.008)
Multi-objective optimization across mass, power, and cost constraints
results: 30 kW cooling and 11 kW heating requirements quantified across 350–400 K atmospheric profile
4 kg passive thermal system meeting $6.3M cost constraint
Design validated against full NASA mission lifecycle requirements

entries.title: SOLAR BOAT POWERTRAIN PACKAGING
entries.org: Stony Brook Solar Boat Racing Team
what: Powertrain packaging for a solar-powered competitive racing boat
Integration and operational readiness for competitive conditions
how: Additive manufacturing for structural packaging investigation
Systematic wet testing and diagnostics for component validation
Robustness-driven design methodology
results: Operational readiness confirmed through systematic wet testing
Robust packaging solution developed via additive manufacturing

entries.title: ROCK VARNISH GEOCHEMICAL PROVENANCE ANALYSIS
what: XRD and XRF investigation of Fe/Mn stratigraphic layering in rock varnish
Provenance analysis of steatite archaeological artifacts
how: X-ray diffraction (2.0–7.5 Å) and XRF elemental mapping
d-spacing fingerprinting for material sourcing
Comparative elemental analysis across 10+ artifact samples
results: Fe/Mn stratigraphic layering identified in 6 samples — consistent with Younger Dryas formation
10+ steatite artifacts traced to Rhode Island quarry sources
10–45× elemental variation distinguishing regional clay sources and trade patterns

entries.title: FIRST ROBOTICS COMPETITION SYSTEMS
what: Competitive robotic system design for FIRST Robotics competitions
Multidisciplinary team collaboration through full engineering design process
how: Iterative mechanical design and prototyping
Cross-functional integration with electrical and software teams
Systematic testing and competition preparation
results: Competition-ready robotic systems designed and deployed
Engineering design process applied through full project lifecycle
